title: Moist Rich Korean Dates Mandu
description: Rich and dense Korean mandu filled with pitted dates and a pasta water emulsion, perfect for a pescatarian friendly meal. This dish is a unique and indulgent twist on traditional Korean cuisine.
instructions: Combine flour and salt in a large mixing bowl and gradually add in water and pasta water to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small balls and roll out each ball into a thin circle.
Place a few pitted dates in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half moon shape and press the edges together to seal the mandu.
Heat sesame oil in a large pan over medium heat and add in the mandu.
Pan fry the mandu until they are golden brown on the bottom, about 2-3 minutes.
Add in soy sauce and green onions and stir to combine.
Serve the mandu hot garnished with toasted sesame seeds.
Repeat the process with the remaining dough and filling.
